title: What Does a Commercial Office Fitout Cost in Newcastle in 2026?
excerpt: Real numbers from the Hunter Valley market — broken down by finish level, category, and base building condition. Not estimates. Not Sydney rates.
author: Joe Kelley
metaTitle: Commercial Fitout Cost Newcastle 2026 | Your Office Space
metaDescription: How much does a commercial office fitout cost in Newcastle in 2026? Real Hunter Valley rates across Basic, Mid-Range and Premium tiers from Your Office Space.
body:
## The honest answer to the most common question we get

Every week we speak with Newcastle business owners who are about to start a fitout and have no idea what it costs. They've been given a Sydney rate by a Sydney firm, or they've been told 'it depends' by someone who doesn't want to commit to a number.

Here are real numbers — benchmarked to the Newcastle and Hunter Valley market as of April 2026.

## The three finish levels

Every fitout falls somewhere on this spectrum:

**Basic** — Functional. Does the job. Best for budget-constrained projects or short-term tenancies. Think carpet tiles, standard partitioning, basic joinery, off-the-shelf furniture.

**Mid-Range** — Professional standard. Quality materials, considered design, proper technology. This is what most growing professional services businesses need.

**Premium** — High-spec. Feature materials, full technology integration, custom joinery, premium furniture. Sends a statement.

## All-in cost ranges for a 250m² Category A fitout

| Category | Basic | Mid-Range | Premium |
|---|---|---|---|
| Pre-construction | $42,000 | $80,000 | $130,000 |
| Structure | $24,000 | $55,000 | $115,000 |
| Finishes | $18,000 | $50,000 | $110,000 |
| Services | $14,000 | $40,000 | $105,000 |
| Technology | $12,000 | $35,000 | $110,000 |
| Furniture | $28,000 | $65,000 | $155,000 |
| Moving & transition | $6,000 | $18,000 | $55,000 |
| **Total (ex GST, inc contingency)** | **~$160,000** | **~$380,000** | **~$870,000** |
| **Per m²** | **~$640/m²** | **~$1,520/m²** | **~$3,480/m²** |

These are Category A base building rates. Shell & Core (blank slab) will be significantly higher. Category B (existing fitout to reuse or strip out) varies — and often surprises.

## The most commonly underbudgeted categories

### Technology
Technology is the category most consistently left out of early fitout estimates — and the one that generates the most complaints when it's done cheaply.

A 250m² mid-range fitout with proper cabling, WiFi, AV in two meeting rooms, access control and a cloud phone system: $35,000–$50,000. Most early estimates assume $10,000.

### Pre-construction
Design, PM, engineers and approvals are paid before a single trade sets foot on site. They feel like an overhead. They are actually the single biggest determinant of whether your construction phase runs to budget.

A good fitout PM saves more than they cost. A poor one — or no one — is how projects blow out by 20–30%.

### Make-good
This is the one that hits at the end. If your lease requires full reinstatement, that's $50–$200/m² when you eventually leave. On a 500m² office, that's $25,000–$100,000 that most business owners have never budgeted for.

Negotiate make-good at lease commencement. It is very hard to change later.

## Newcastle vs Sydney — the real gap

Sydney CBD fitout rates run approximately 30–40% above Newcastle and Hunter Valley equivalents. If a Sydney-based contractor or designer quotes you using their standard rates, adjust accordingly.

Local contractors and suppliers — who understand the local supply chain, local trades, and local conditions — typically deliver better value for Hunter Region projects.
